factors.summary: The company's critical accounting estimates remain unchanged and are discussed with the Audit Committee.

evidence.text: There have been no material changes in our critical accounting estimates during the current period. For a detailed discussion of other significant accounting policies that may involve a higher degree of judgment, refer to our Form 10-K.

factors.summary: The filing includes forward-looking statements that are subject to risks and uncertainties.

evidence.text: This filing contains “forward-looking statements” within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, pursuant to the safe harbor provisions of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 (the “Act”).
evidence.subsection: Cautionary Statement Regarding Forward-Looking Statements and Information

factors.summary: There have been no material changes in the company's risk factors, which are detailed in the Form 10-K.

evidence.text: There have been no material changes in our risk factors from those disclosed in Part I, Item 1a. Risk Factors of our Form 10-K.

factors.summary: Overall demand appears to be decelerating, particularly in the ABL segment.

evidence.text: Net sales for the second quarter of fiscal 2024 decreased $37.7 million, or 4.0%, to $905.9 million, compared with $943.6 million in the prior-year period due to a decline in sales within our ABL segment, partially offset by higher sales within our ISG segment.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2023

factors.summary: The backlog appears to have been worked through, leading to a decrease in net sales.

evidence.text: The second quarter of 2023 benefited from working through an elevated backlog.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2023

factors.summary: The company is facing some headwinds in pricing, as evidenced by the decline in net sales despite favorable material costs.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin increased 240 basis points to 45.5% from 43.1% compared with the prior-year period. Our gross profit increased compared with the prior period due primarily to favorable material and import costs, partially offset by the fall through of the net sales decline as well as higher quality, labor, and overhead costs.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2023

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly ISG, which is contributing to improved margins.

evidence.text: ISG operating profit margin increased 260 basis points to 13.4% from 10.8% compared with the prior-year period.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 2. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, as evidenced by the decline in net sales in the ABL segment.

evidence.text: Net sales for the second quarter of fiscal 2024 decreased $37.7 million, or 4.0%, to $905.9 million, compared with $943.6 million in the prior-year period due to a decline in sales within our ABL segment, partially offset by higher sales within our ISG segment.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2023

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining, particularly in the ABL segment, leading to lower revenue.

evidence.text: ABL net sales for the second quarter of fiscal 2024 decreased $47.3 million, or 5.3%, to $843.5 million, compared with $890.8 million in the prior-year period. Sales within the ABL segment decreased due to lower net sales across all channels.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 2. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

factors.summary: The ISG segment is growing faster, driven by higher sales and contributions from the KE2 Therm acquisition.

evidence.text: ISG net sales for the second quarter of fiscal 2024 increased $9.9 million, or 17.0%, to $68.1 million, compared with $58.2 million in the prior-year period. This increase was due primarily to contributions from higher net sales, partially offset by higher employee-related costs.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 2. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

factors.summary: Input costs are favorable, contributing to higher gross profits despite lower sales.

evidence.text: Gross profit for the second quarter of fiscal 2024 increased $5.7 million, or 1.4%, to $412.4 million, compared with $406.7 million in the prior-year period, and gross profit margin increased 240 basis points to 45.5% from 43.1% compared with the prior-year period. Our gross profit increased compared with the prior period due primarily to favorable material and import costs.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2023

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding, driven by favorable material costs and lower sales-related expenses.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin increased 240 basis points to 45.5% from 43.1% compared with the prior-year period. Operating profit increased $6.6 million, or 5.9%, to $118.1 million.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2023

factors.summary: The company has made strategic acquisitions, such as KE2 Therm, which are contributing to growth in the ISG segment.

evidence.text: On May 15, 2023, using cash on hand, we acquired all of the equity interests of KE2 Therm Solutions, Inc. (''KE2 Therm''). KE2 Therm develops and provides intelligent refrigeration control solutions that deliver the precision of digital controls to promote safety, efficiency, and reliability, while delivering cost savings to the customer. This acquisition is intended to expand ISG's technology and controls product portfolio and reach new customers.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 2. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable, with cash on hand increasing and cash flows from operations supporting capital allocation priorities.

evidence.text: Cash generated from operating activities and cash on hand were used during the current year to fund our capital allocation priorities as discussed below. We generated $292.6 million of cash flows from operating activities during the six months ended February 29, 2024, compared to $306.4 million in the prior-year period, a decrease of $13.8 million.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 2. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

factors.summary: The company is maintaining a stable debt position, with cash on hand and borrowing capacity supporting liquidity.

evidence.text: At February 29, 2024, our outstanding debt balance was $495.9 million, which consisted solely of our Unsecured Notes, compared to our cash position of $578.9 million. We were in compliance with all covenants under our financing arrangements as of February 29, 2024.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 2. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its shareholder return policy, with consistent dividend payments and share repurchases.

evidence.text: We paid dividends on our common stock of $8.8 million ($0.28 per share) and $8.5 million ($0.26 per share) during the six months ended February 29, 2024 and February 28, 2023, respectively. During the first six months of fiscal 2024, we repurchased 0.4 million shares of our outstanding common stock for $67.6 million.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 2. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

factors.summary: Construction activity is affecting demand, particularly during winter months and annual budget cycles.

evidence.text: Our business exhibits some seasonality, with net sales being affected by weather and seasonal demand on construction and installation programs, particularly during the winter months, as well as the annual budget cycles of major customers.
